title: Local-first model runtime readiness and escalation policy
problem: Ticket-037/038 implemented live Ollama structured tasks and health/smoke gating, but operators lack a single local-first runbook, task-tier assignment table, and escalation policy. model-health reports model_available=false without actionable guidance when the configured tag is missing. No cloud escalation rules are documented.
acceptance_criteria: Operator can follow docs to reach model-health with model_available=true (pull or alias paths documented).
Escalation policy states mock default for CI/tests; local Ollama for real research; cloud disabled until future ticket.
Task tier table maps human intent to current modules and marks gaps as future local tasks.
model-health JSON includes configured_model, available_models sample, and action_hint when model missing.
Golden tests and CI remain mock-only; no OpenAI/OpenRouter code added.
Models never write accepted records / never shell/Git section matches 10_SAFETY_MODEL.md.
non_goals: Do not implement OpenAI or OpenRouter adapters.
Do not run live smoke tests unless explicitly approved.
Do not add API keys or commit .env secrets.
Do not make live LLM mode the default.
Do not wire cloud escalation gates in Python (ticket-059+).
rollback_plan: Revert docs and model-health hint fields; no schema or export changes.
